entry.title: Re: Exploring SAP Joule Integration Options with SAP Data...
entry.content:
Hi,

Currently the Early Adapter Care program is going on for Joule in Datasphere: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/become-an-early-adopter-for-joule-in-sap-datasphere/ba-p/14096474 

Joule in SAC is in Roadmap for this year in H2.

Joule integration with other solutions, refer to https://help.sap.com/docs/JOULE/6189c8655c484916bb8eb767126a653a/3b797193d7694d7492ca1203c2b21dee.html 

Thanks,

Shailendar.

entry.title: Re: My Introduction
entry.content:
Hey Souravraj welcome! happy to have you in the SAP Community ^^

You can check the location groups out and find the one closer to you and learn while making cool connections. You can also follow the topics that you find helpful on the managed tags page.

Have fun :))

entry.title: Re: Adding mail text in email - IS-U invoice through Email

It seems - the subject line is fetched from the composite element's (the element I'm using to bind both of the application form) description.

entry.title: Re: Rehire scenario with EC mini master
entry.content: you can use the custom MDF table with terminated employees,  and call this during Hire check especially using unique fields like birth date or national id along with first name and last name.

entry.title: Re: Technical Error in PCC
entry.content: what is the beginning error, it ll give more information or check the ST22 error for more details

entry.title: Reigniting Purpose Through SAP Certification
entry.content:
Yasir Sheikh, an SAP Project Manager based in Dubai, has spent more than two decades leading ERP transformations across global firms like IBM, PwC, Deloitte, BearingPoint, and EY. With a career dedicated to bridging business and IT, Yasir has always been passionate about delivering high-impact ERP transformations — and equally committed to continuously upskilling himself along the way. “What drives me most is creating real value for clients while continuously upskilling myself.”

Staying Ahead

In his current role, Yasir leads implementations of SAP Fieldglass and SAP S/4HANA Cloud, managing teams and aligning delivery with clients across regions. “My typical day involves managing project teams, aligning delivery plans with clients, resolving cross-functional issues, and ensuring that SAP solutions are implemented effectively and on time.” Right now, he’s leading a multi-country SAP Fieldglass solution implementation project for a financial institution in the UAE, from planning to post-go-live support.

With cloud adoption accelerating, Yasir saw the need to deepen his knowledge, even after 22 years in the field. He chose the Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud (Public Edition) Certification to align his hands-on expertise with the latest innovations, and to strengthen his ability to meet client expectations in a cloud-first world. “Although I had extensive experience managing SAP projects, I wanted to update my knowledge to match SAP’s evolving cloud-first strategy,” he explained. “I also wanted to show that at 49, it’s never too late to reinvest in your skills — a message I hope can inspire others in the industry.”

Learning That Connects Directly to Work

Learning something new while managing live client projects wasn’t easy. But Yasir embraced the challenge. “Balancing active client delivery with late-night study sessions required discipline, but the structure of the free SAP Learning site and the relevance of the learning content made it easier to stay motivated.” The learning paid off immediately — not just in knowledge, but in practical application.

“One of the most energizing aspects was applying new knowledge immediately in my real-world projects, for example connecting cloud configuration concepts to actual client discussions.”

New Projects, New Purpose

Completing the certification exam brought both professional and personal rewards. It enhanced Yasir’s credibility with clients, created new career opportunities, and reminded him of why he loves what he does. “It gave me a renewed sense of professional purpose, and reaffirmed my belief that learning is a lifelong journey,” he shares. "It also sparked a desire to give back, to contribute more to the SAP community, and mentor younger professionals."

At this stage of his career, Yasir sees continued learning as essential — not just for growth, but for relevance. “Staying relevant, sharing knowledge, and proving that learning has no age barrier are most important to me.”

Are you ready to begin your SAP journey?  Explore the SAP Learning site and take the first step to transform your future.

entry.title: Where does SAP Datasphere store the business names of columns for SQL access?
entry.content:
I crreated a database user to access data directly via SQL for an external system. It works but I only get the technical names for each column.

Where does SAP Datasphere store the texts for the business names exactly? I found several views that looked promising but none of them contained the business names:

If anybody knows more, please let me know!

Thanks, Alexander.

entry.title: Re: No certification even after a week of exam and support ticket
entry.content: 3 weeks now. No certification yet. Having tried multiple options (including email to e-learning) any other recommendations to escalate this further. Thanks in advance.

entry.title: 🚛 Migration vs. ⬆️ Upgrade: Understanding the SAP Essentials (With Real‑World Clarity!)
entry.content:
Ever heard someone say "We're upgrading to S/4HANA" when they really meant “migrating” to HANA? You’re not alone—these terms are often misunderstood, but knowing the difference is critical 💡. 

Whether you're a consultant, a technical architect, or part of a functional team, or Business users understanding the difference is crucial for planning, execution, and business alignment. Knowing the difference between migration and upgradation can help you communicate better, plan smarter, and execute projects more efficiently.

 

🏗️ Migration = Moving House

You're shifting your entire SAP landscape—platform, database, and deployment environment.
Common Scenarios:

  • On‑Prem Oracle → SAP HANA

  • On‑Prem → Cloud

  • Legacy ERP → S/4HANA (Greenfield)

Tools: SAP Migration Cockpit, DMO, LSMW, Data Services
Focus: Data quality, performance, scalability

🛠️ Upgrade = Renovation

Same house, but with brand‑new features and updates.
Typical Use‑Cases:

  • ECC 6.0 EHP5 → EHP8

  • NetWeaver, GUI → Fiori UX

  • ECC → S/4HANA via Brownfield

Tools: SUM, Maintenance Planner, SPAM/SAINT
Focus: Functionality, user experience, compliance

🔍 Quick Comparison

Factor Migration Upgrade
GoalNew platformNew version
Change TypeTech + functionalFunctional only
Data HandlingETL / cleansed loadRetain existing data
Risk LevelHigherLower
When to Choose?DB switch, greenfieldNew features, UX, support
 

🎯 Which One Do You Need?

  • Migration = Changing where or how your SAP runs

  • Upgrade = Improving what’s already there

👉 Why It Matters

  • Align teams: set clear technical vs. business objectives

  • Optimize planning: scope, budget, risk & tool choice

  • Improve communication: speak the same language across IT & business

📣 From the SAP Community

Have you tackled a migration or upgrade?

  • What was your biggest challenge—data cleanup, downtime, custom code?

  • Any tools or tips you swear by?

Drop your insights below—let’s build a resource together! 👇

📲 Let's Connect

Follow me for more SAP & S/4HANA insights, and reach out if you want to dive deeper!
LinkedIn: www.linkedin.com/in/mirza-hasan-051369231

entry.title: Despite creation of a spatial record via the API, service region is not visible on Service Map
entry.content:
Dear Community,

I am setting up Service Regions in order to make them appear visually on the Service Map, as described in the following SAP Help Documentation: Field Service Management - Planning and Dispatching - Regions

I have created several regions:

I can their internal id via a CoreSQL query:

I have used Postman to create a Spatial Record, as described in the following SAP Help Documentation: Field Service Management - FSM Spatial Data API - Create Spatial Record. I used exactly the same body as in the documentation, only changing the id of the region. I got a 201 response - created:

So far so good, but when I go to the Service Map, I cannot see the "Eye" icon which is visible on  this picture, which is coming from the official SAP documentation. 

 

I am using Google Maps as Map Provider. 

Have anyone experienced this issue ? Am I missing a configuration step ? 

Thank you for your help !

entry.title: Time sheet working time and absence time are colliding but no admin alert
entry.content:
HI All , 

Noticed this weird behavior for time sheet and absence where following sequence of event has happened.

Employee was in 7-16:30 shift on 24th Jun 2024. 

Applied for half day leave - 13:00 - 16:30 leave created. 

Working time came from biometric on 25th June for 24th June for 5:52- 13:00

On 8th July 2024 - One of the admin assigned Back dated Temporary working schedule  for 6:00- 15:30. 

Time alert generated that absence is falling out of planned time which makes sense. 

13th Jan 2025 - Admin adjusted the absence within planned time somehow. Not sure how but they were able to save 10:00 - 15:30 based on temp. work schedule half day.  which dissolved the timealert.

Suddenly in may , the timesheet is recalculated for backdate and alert is clear for absence. but on UI THIS BELOW ERROR APPEARS ON UI. -

No other alert is generated for ADMIN . How can we track such errors if these are only appearing on UI.

How one can ensure collision on absence and attendance are tracked ?

Is this a product gap or limitation or bug ?

entry.title: Re: In sourcing and procurement, Output determination for...

entry.title: Re: Practice-systems/basic-abap-programming NOT FOUND (404)
entry.content:
Hi @kendel @Jessica45 

If you run into issue during enrollment or practice in the learning system please create a new case ticketing system 
Please make sure to select "Practice Systems" as the “Service Category” and kindly fill out the appropriate form.
When raising an inquiry, include detailed information in the description to help our team assist you better.
Please also check our Practice systems support where you find a list of general questions and answers on practice systems, all designed to help you to resolve issues may encounter on SAP Learning.

Kind regards
Margit

 

Kind regards

Margit

entry.title: Re: Getting 403 forbidden when trying to return with cart...
entry.content: @rajeshps The transaction for the service is SICF. The HOOK_URL value has to be configured in the call structure of the catalog, with the correct Fiori server name and port. This will be used to return to the Fiori app from the external catalog.

entry.content:

I read the following article:

2442595 - Error:"The object you are trying to promote may be available in the Recycle Bin. Delete the object from the Recycle Bin and try again" when promoting object | SAP Knowledge Base Article

but it didn't help.

I'm working on SAP BusinessObjects BI 4.3 Support Pack 2 Patch 12.

Thank you so much in advance for your help.

Francesco

entry.title: How to restrict access to view of supplier invoices in S4Hana Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dears,

As it is in subject - How to restrict access to view of supplier invoices in S4Hana Public Cloud ?

For purpose:

Persons on B2B contract - they can't see each other's invoices in Supplier Invoice List app. 

Both person will be in the same Company Code, in the same Purchasing Group. Only difference will be in Cost Center.

How can we avoid this kind of situations ? 

Which attribute can limit this?

Regards

entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

Why is there a need to create a purchasing info record before a supplier return process can be carried out in SAP S/4HANA public cloud (as this is a prerequisite). If this isn't done, the system would be able to generate an outbound delivery ID which would be used for the picking and GI processes.

Can anyone help out with this.

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: Treating Business Goals as "Individual" Goals possible?
entry.content:
Hi @xavierlegarrec , 

in the end there are only 2 values for each employee that need to be calculated with / or against the target amount. 

Lets say the target percentage is 10% (which is valid for all employees that are eligible) I need to divide it into 2 parts. 

a) "Billable Hours" part

The billable hours can be retrieved from CompInfo where are 2 seperate fields available. I described the calculation for that in my last answer.

b) "Individual Goal" part

Needs to be the second part of the calculation

 

Now it might get tricky:

W for weightage ) The tricky thing is that the weightage between a) and b) was determined at the end of last cycle and needs to be taken into consideration. Is there a way to determine the weightage of the sections on an employee level for example

Employee A with a) 30% and b) 70%

Employee B with a) 75% and b) 25%

?

Thank you!

(P.S.: as you can see, i don't have a lot of experience and i have to admit, my certification was some time ago, I obviously need some guidance ...)

entry.title: How to filter in the trash in Manager ?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

Can you please help me out with how to filter in the trash in Manager? I can see the items that were deleted appearing there but I cant filter because it's by chronological order and not alphabetical. 

I tried adding a custom filter but the filtering is only within "Task filters" that we havent used for every object that has been deleted. There is no "view deleted items" status or "deleted" status so there is no way I can filter within the trash.

Additionally some resources have been deleted and are polluting the trash as well and there is no way to restore them and clicking on "revert resources" doesnt make it disappear and most of the time I get an error message.

Appreciate the help!!

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Mass Creation of Purchase Orders （6VW)
entry.content:
Hi @Li_Grace 

I got it. From your screenshot, the error is related to the Item Category field being blank in the Excel template for Purchase Order upload (most likely using Mass Upload in S/4HANA Public Cloud).

Even though the system may default the item category for a standard PO item (typically, "blank" means standard), the template expects an explicit entry or recognises it incorrectly when left blank.

What to do:

  • In your Excel template under Item Category, input a space character (" ") or leave it empty without any formatting or hidden characters.
  • If that still fails, explicitly enter 0 or blank (depends on system version, but in many Public Cloud tenants, 0 = standard).
  • Alternatively, try entering "Standard" or use dropdown values, if available, to match system expectations.

Check the Fiori app or documentation tied to your PO upload template , sometimes the expected values are not truly blank but require a code like "0" or "standard".

If it still fails, feel free to share the full error message pop-up or line comment from the upload log,I can pinpoint exactly what’s failing.

With regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: Changing User Status With STATUS_CHANGE_EXTERN
entry.content: Do you know which specific Business Transaction need to be define?

entry.title: Re: SAP BW InfoObject 0DEB_CRE_LC with +/- sign ?
entry.content:
Hi Charlie

if i remember correctly the SIGN flag in infobject maintenance refers to BEX query data display. In the dso you will see the signs of every record. 

hope it helps

entry.title: Re: Validation rule for Asset Master Data
entry.content:
Hi @JAS3311 

Not sure about Public Cloud but in On Premise and Private cloud SAP GUI, you can add missing fields through transaction code SM30 and enter Table - VWTYGB01 

Check for the relevant Table and Field and deactivate the “Exclude” checkbox, and SAVE the setting. You can add your field if its not available.

After that Run program RGUGBR00 in SE38

Check if same can be done through any views in SAP Public cloud.

Please check and confirm

Regards

Vijaykumar

entry.title: Joule for ABAP-Developers: An unexpected error occured
entry.content:
Hi there,

since several days Joule for ABAP development stopped to work on all of our BTP ABAP instances (paid and free tier). 

When chatting with Joule, the following error appears in the Joule view of Eclipse:

When trying to generate unit tests, Joule responds initially, but then refuses to generate the tests:

Also the chat when generating a transactional RAP business object fails:

However, the AI powered code suggestions works as usual. 

Eclipse and ABAP development tools are on the latest release version.

Does anybody experience the same issues? How to fix this?

Thank you & BR
Stefan

entry.title: error 314 numeric overflow for table containing value 1.0
entry.content:
Dear experts,
When I try to run a dataflow, I get the error below. Could you guide me on how to solve the issue ? 

Group messages:
Group: default; Messages: Graph failure: target1 failed with the following error:
General error;314 numeric overflow: Failed in "Column_Name" column with the value 1.000000000000000000
Process(es) terminated with error(s). restartOnFailure==false

In the data flow I want to run, data source contains "1.0" in the column (instead of "1.000000000000000000") and then converted to the local table in the target after projection.

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Uploading Attachments’ – Attach any File (PDF/DOC/JPG/XLS/TXT..) through the OData
entry.content: Excellent Blog! The entire block of code is working without any error.

entry.title: Re: SAP Signavio: How to Convert yyyy-mm-ddThh:mm:ss (String) to yyyy-mm-dd hh:mm:ss (Timestamp)
entry.content:
Hi,

without checking out your data set, I am assuming "OH"."createddate" has the value 'Y' in at least one case and not the value '[Y]' which you are filtering in line 4. Maybe check your data set and adjust your code in line 4 accordingly.

Further line 4 could be replaced with something more generic like:

WHEN NOT("OH"."createddate" LIKE '____-__-__T__:__:__%') THEN NULL

Hope this helps.

Best,

Benedict

entry.title: Re: 🌟 Community Spotlight: Our Top Contributors May 2025 🌟
entry.content:
@AnnePetteroe 

Congratulations to all contributors. 👍

entry.title: SAP Stammtisch Dresden - Let's Talk about Joule!
entry.content:
The local SAP Community in Dresden has a chance to meet and discuss whatever is on your mind about SAP Joule, Business Data Cloud, any technical topics like CAP, RAP, just whatever is on you mind.  Your host will be @josh_bentley so come meet, take a pic, or just share some stories with Josh as this is his first time in Dresden!

SAP Stammtisch in Dresden will happen on June 17th, 2025 at 18:00 local time for a few hours, so join us and be a part of our Group!  You don't have to be an attendee of any other local SAP event, just someone active or curious about the SAP Community and our focus on developers and technical conversations.

Let’s talk about what's new in the SAP world, about interesting SAP projects in the region, and what's new with ourselves.

Please let us know if you will participate in this SAP Stammtisch by clicking "I will attend", the location is a restaurant called Watzke am Goldenen Reiter  in downtown Dresden near the Golden Reiter statue.  Be sure to bring a jacket in case there isn't enough room inside for our group.

FYI, everyone is responsible for their own cost to buy a food/drink item if they wish, we are just happy for the chance to connect in person and have some relaxing conversation!

entry.title: Re: Pro-rate salaried teammates wages
entry.content: Proration by date is only done by calendar days, not work days.  The only way to prorate by work days is to import the appropriate percentage.

entry.title: Re: How to create the transformation to the Hierarchy of...

Roland

entry.title: Re: Promotion Functionality in SuccessFactors Compensation
entry.content: Unfortunately, I cannot troubleshoot your issue based on a screenshot. I would need to access the system and look at all the settings on the template and the XML. If you need to have additional support, you can open a ticket or engage a SAP Salesperson to begin a Professional Services engagement.

entry.title: Re: SAP Subscription Billing
entry.content: I had the same question before—turns out you can handle usage-based and recurring charges separately by setting up different rate plans and using charge types in the subscription product.

entry.title: RFOPENPOSTING_FR functionality
entry.content:
Hello,

Can any one please explian the functionality of RFOPENPOSTING_FR transaction. I understand from the program documentation that is used to create the FEC file and this program posts the opening balance of the closed fiscal year in the next fiscal year.

But does this program post only the closing balances of Customer and Vendor reconciliation account or all the balance sheet GLs?

Any help would be appreciated.

 

Thanks

Prasad Garude.

entry.title: Re: SAP C4C V2 Extension Scenarios on Custom Fields
entry.content: Hello Molly, I couldn't find it in the road map. Which quarter should I check?

entry.title: (Datasphere/SAC) Time Dimension attributes not visible in SAC
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I have created an analytical model in Datasphere (DSP). In the underlying fact view, I have created a handful of associations, including an association with the time dimension table created for the space.

Enabling all of the attributes in the DSP Analytical model allows me to see and use them in the Analytical model Preview, SAC Data Analyzer, and in a Table Widget in a SAC story.

But alas! I need them in a chart, where they are not visible. Only the hierarchy I created in the time dimension is available when adding the time dimension to the chart.

entry.title: CORUPROC transaction not clearing AFFW data
entry.content:
We have several MESSAGE_TYPE_X errors on a daily.
We checked some SAP notes that indicate the execution of CORUPROC with the Goods Movements flags activated to solve the problem.

We have a lot of old records in AFFW, and our understanding was that the CORUPROC with execution parameters flagged :
- include Failed Goods Movements
- Automatic goods receipt
- Backflush
Would delete the failed records from AFFW and resolve the TYPE_X dumps.

We run the JOB daily, but the AFFW still has the same records and errors.
Can any of you gurus help?

entry.title: paging in RAP service - Data is getting copied from 2nd page to first page
entry.content:
May be this is minor thing which we might missing but unable to resolve -

Using RAP development created SMART tile to get details of BP.
Requirement is to have two pages based on vendor sub range for example -

 

BPVendor Sub BP1Vendor Sub BP2
ABCSBP-1SBP-2

So on First page will contain all details for Vendor SBP-1 and on second page will have all details of SBP-2 

Customer name which is referring same filed at table level (LFA1-LSTNR )  is added in both screen.

Issue is - 

On first screen customer name is getting displayed correct at first , and on second page also getting displayed for SBP-2 , but when we click on back button SBP-a customer name is getting replaced wit SBP-2 name .
For maore details

First page 

Second page 

 

Clicking back page 2000 getting replaced with 1000

Thank in advance 
ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model SAP Fiori

entry.title: Re: Items not appearing in library despite being uploaded into LMS for more than 72hrs
entry.content: Hi, Could you please share how this issue was resolved?. We are having the same issue in our environment. The library item is available in the old learning home but not available in the new experience even after 72 hours. Thank you..

entry.title: SAP TM - How does the Optimizer Planning plans the Freight Unit type?
entry.content: In our Scenario, we have three Freight Unit type, Domestic, International and Stock Transfer Order. How does it know which one to create during the creation of Outbound Delivery Document? Country? Scheduling/Route?

entry.title: Re: Adding mail text in email - IS-U invoice through Email
entry.content: We have created user exit in collection application form where we define subject and enter in in c-mail_title.

entry.title: Re: How to Set an Image as Background in a Page of an SAP...
entry.content: MDK does not support setting an image as the background of a page.  We do support an Image Section if you want to display an image with other content on the page.

entry.title: Re: Base taxonomy 2024 for ESEF scenario in SAP DM
entry.content: Hi Guido, is there any update on the base taxonomy for 2024/2025? Thank you in advance! Regards Peter

entry.title: Re: Pro-rate salaried teammates wages
entry.content: The import would be via the UDF unless there is a field in EC that can be mapped. There is no need to import anything for employees who are at 100% as the system will default 100% proration for a blank value. Those who are prorated 5 of 7 days for example, would have an import value of 71.4 (which is 5 divided by 7). You can also import a prorated start date and prorated end date and have the system calculate the number of days but that number would include weekends as well. The import keys are: salaryProrating for importing a perecentage, and salaryProratingStartDate and salaryProratingEndDate if you are importing specific dates.

entry.title: Re: Datasphere's task chain are not triggered after recent updates on HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Error occured at multiple tenants today again. Oss note logged.

Let's hope they fix this soon..

entry.title: SMA Solar relies on SAP Cloud Application Services for greater independence in energy supply
entry.content:
During the IT Online Conference held from January 27-30, 2025 under the theme "S/4HANA Transformation and Innovation with SAP RISE and GROW", Heiko Kehm, Head of IT Order to Cash at SMA Solar Technology AG (SMA), provided valuable insights into his company's digitalization strategy. In a discussion with Maike Rose from the IT Online Magazine, as well as Claudia Johne (Client Delivery Manager) and Patricia Gantert (Business Development Manager), both from SAP, Kehm explained to conference attendees how SMA uses SAP Cloud Application Services to accelerate and simplify its digital transformation.

Before the actual interview, conference attendees were asked to answer two questions. Here are the results:

To prepare for the webinar, the three interviewees each formulated a thesis regarding SAP Cloud Application Services. Maike addressed these theses in her introductory moderation and presented them to the audience.

Heiko Kehm:

Support in application operations through SAP Cloud Application Services helps companies to relieve their employees and focus on the digitalization of their business processes. This enables the full potential of cloud investments to be realized faster and with better results.

Claudia Johne:

SAP Cloud Application Services are designed to provide optimized value for customers. The Application Services Manager ensures that services are delivered efficiently, reliably, and tailored to customer needs.

Patricia Gantert:

What do SAP Cloud Application Services have to do with sports? In a gym, a competent team ensures that the equipment works perfectly, allowing trainees to fully concentrate on their training plan. Similarly, SAP Cloud Application Services focus on flexible and simplified service use, thereby relieving employees within the company.

Background and Company Profile

SMA Solar is a leading provider of photovoltaic system technology and energy solutions based in Niestetal near Kassel. Founded in 1981, the company employs around 4,000 people worldwide and achieved a turnover of approximately €1.9 billion in 2023.

SMA offers a comprehensive portfolio for all segments of solar power generation, storage, and delivery - from solutions for private households to industrial applications. This includes photovoltaic inverters, storage solutions, charging solutions for e-mobility, and energy management services and apps.

From Digital Vision to SMA Solar's Digital Transformation Journey

A digital future vision was developed as the basis for SMA's digitalization strategy. This vision considers processes, data, applications, technologies, and people—both employees and managers—in a holistic cooperation model. The aim is to eliminate departmental and content-related barriers and drive digital transformation forward.

Additionally, important guidelines were set for the company to provide a framework for the path to the digital target vision. These include:

  • Standardization of processes
  • Compatibility in the form of plug-and-play
  • Speed to secure competitiveness, to react flexibly to changes, and to innovate in the market
  • A process-oriented work approach to improve customer satisfaction
  • Strong partnerships to focus on core competencies

The digital target vision, process phases, and key success factors of SMA at a glance:

Since 2015, SMA has been pursuing a consistent cloud-first strategy. Important milestones include:

  • Migration of the SAP landscape to the HANA Enterprise Cloud
  • Introduction of SaaS solutions such as SAP SuccessFactors
  • Implementation of SAP S/4HANA as the digital core from 2018
  • Transition to RISE with SAP in 2022
  • Introduction of Digital Manufacturing in electronic production in 2023

Today, SMA is almost 100% in the cloud and no longer operates its own data centre.

What Exactly Are SAP Cloud Application Services?

This question was explained by expert Claudia Johne. SAP Cloud Application Services offer customers comprehensive support in operating their applications and processes. As an integral part of the global SAP support network, they provide a variety of services. Over 1,500 certified consultants have access to the latest product knowledge. Even during the setup and implementation of SAP solutions, questions often arise about operating the SAP landscape and ensuring the stability of the system landscape. SAP Cloud Application Services also support security or cybersecurity topics, test management, development, or improvement of the SAP stack.

SAP Cloud Application Services support the following areas:

  • Ensuring the stability, reliability, and security of applications, extensions, and integrations
  • Taking over all reactive and proactive operational tasks so that the customer can focus on their core business
  • Faster testing and deployment for quicker introduction of SAP solutions
  • Deployment and operation of new features and technologies

How Does SMA Solar use SAP Cloud Application Services?

SMA has been using SAP Cloud Application Services (formerly Application Management Services) for over 10 years. According to Heiko Kehm, the main reasons for this are:

  • Cost flexibility instead of fixed cost structures
  • Relief of the internal IT team from routine tasks
  • High quality and stability in operations
  • Overall responsibility of SAP for product, operations, and consulting
  • Trustworthy partnership for problem-solving
  • Focus on core competencies and digital innovation

Claudia Johne confirmed these specific benefits mentioned by SMA and added the following characteristics of SAP Cloud Application Services:

  • Comprehensive application management, i.e., support in managing cross-SAP solutions, from SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud to Public Cloud solutions like SAP SuccessFactors or SAP Business Technology Platform.
  • Close cooperation within the internal SAP network.
  • Proactive solution management, e.g., to comply with EU regulations.
  • Extensive language offerings beyond the standard languages of English and German.
  • A central Application Services Manager ensures comprehensive application management, i.e., the customer receives everything from one source.
  • "SAP runs SAP", i.e., SAP also uses the same tools, processes, and best practices within SAP.

Specifically, SMA uses the following packages of SAP Cloud Application Services:

  • Incident, problem, and change management
  • Application operations for SAP S/4HANA operations
  • Application monitoring (interfaces, jobs, certificates)
  • Security management (implementation of security notes)
  • Support for upgrades and migrations

For Which Customers Is the Use of SAP Cloud Application Services Recommended?

To answer this question, Claudia Johne provided some practical use cases where she recommends the use of SAP Cloud Application Services:

  • Customers who do not have in-house support expertise in certain applications or whose employees are long-term tied up in projects can be relieved.
  • Often, customers have guided implementations, i.e., they have engaged SAP or a partner for the implementation phase and are looking for a follow-up model for operating their SAP landscape.
  • In hybrid landscapes, customers may want to focus on their SAP S/4HANA landscape. In this case, SAP Cloud Application Services can support the operation of Public Cloud solutions, for example.
  • When it comes to the strategic development of the system landscape, i.e., the question "Where should it go? What should still be achieved?", the customer is usually on their own. Here, too, SAP Cloud Application Services can help and relieve the company.

How does collaboration around SAP Cloud Application Services work?

According to Heiko Kehm, collaboration in practice is organized through defined roles and regular alignment:

  • Weekly meetings between SMA Service Manager and SAP Application Service Manager
  • Weekly exchange between SMA Inhouse Consulting and SAP Support Team
  • Close coordination with SAP Account Management on contractual and strategic issues

Technically, ticket processing takes place via an interface between the SMA Jira system and the SAP Solution Manager.

From SAP's perspective, Claudia Johne added to the collaboration and her specific role as follows:

The Application Service Manager is the direct point of contact regarding Cloud Application Services. This includes responsibility for delivery performance management, which includes delivery escalation issues or coordination regarding operational and planning activities, for example, if SMA is interested in new Cloud Application Services.

The cooperation model at a glance:

What are the key Success Factors for Digital Transformation?

To conclude the discussion, Maike Rose asked the participants to name one or two key factors for the success of a digital transformation. The factors mentioned included:

  • Focus on innovation and market speed
  • Increasing importance of IT as a key component of the business model
  • Creating innovation spaces through stable operations
  • Early consideration of Cloud Application Services for support
  • Importance of open and cooperative collaboration

Finally, some practical tips from first-hand experience...

  • Analyse which Cloud Application Services make sense for your company
  • Define clear roles and responsibilities in the collaboration
  • Establish regular coordination rounds at operational and strategic levels
  • Use the flexibility to expand or reduce services as needed
  • Actively accompany the change process within the company to create acceptance

Final Thoughts

The use of SAP Cloud Application Services enables SMA Solar to focus on the further development of its core business and digital transformation.

By relieving routine tasks and accessing SAP expertise, the company can implement innovations faster and respond flexibly to market changes. A trustworthy partnership and clear governance structures are decisive success factors.

What are the key Success Factors for Digital Transformation?

To conclude the discussion, Maike Rose asked the participants to name one or two key factors for the success of a digital transformation. The factors mentioned included:

  • Focus on innovation and market speed
  • Increasing importance of IT as a key component of the business model
  • Creating innovation spaces through stable operations
  • Early consideration of Cloud Application Services for support
  • Importance of open and cooperative collaboration

Finally, some practical tips from first-hand experience...

  • Analyse which Cloud Application Services make sense for your company
  • Define clear roles and responsibilities in the collaboration
  • Establish regular coordination rounds at operational and strategic levels
  • Use the flexibility to expand or reduce services as needed
  • Actively accompany the change process within the company to create acceptance

Final Thoughts

The use of SAP Cloud Application Services enables SMA Solar to focus on the further development of its core business and digital transformation.

By relieving routine tasks and accessing SAP expertise, the company can implement innovations faster and respond flexibly to market changes. A trustworthy partnership and clear governance structures are decisive success factors.

The webinar was summarized by Sandra Schulze in a Graphical Recording.

Further Information:

SAP Cloud Application Services on sap.com

Adoption Infographic featuring SAP Preferred Success, SAP Cloud Application Services und SAP Learning Hub

If you have any questions, please feel free to contact us at:

sap_cloud_acceleration_mee@sap.com

claudia.johne@sap.com

patricia.gantert@sap.com

entry.title: Re: Practice-systems/basic-abap-programming NOT FOUND (404)
entry.content: sorry i hit send before i finished, it would be great to speak with you live if possible, its not that simple. the system was there, the links worked, my class was working in it and then poof they were gone, see this linnk please https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/acquire-core-abap-skills?url_id=text-gl-lh if you click it and scroll down, you will see the hands-on-practice boxes now say "hands-on-practice for undefined" and the link provides a 404 error. But then i went to google and found this link https://learning.sap.com/practice-systems/abap-programming-for-sap-hana when my licensed, subsribed instructor and class went it to, it sits at wait 90minutes for preparation and it never comes up, we have waited 24 hours now. so if someone could read this entirely, and actually look at what i am saying and try it, you will see the issues. i raised a ticket, many of us did and all we get back from the ticket center is that we need a subscription, which everyone in the class has, so... please someone call me 561-445-9534 or email me directly at jessica.coyle@capgemini.com please no more "canned" responses about ticket centers, please read it and reply to what i wrote

entry.title: Subcontracting with Chargeable Components
entry.content:
Hello every one,

I have activated the Subcontracting process with chargeable components, and it's working properly. but when I'm posting the component to the subcontractor with 541 MVT  from other plant under the same Company Code, the FI is not generated. for example:
Plant X1 and Plant X2 under the same Company Code AAAA, the PO is created in Plant X1 and the finish good will received in plant X1 but the component will Issued from X2. in this case the fi document is not generated.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: Deployments without passwords with SAP Continuous Integration and Delivery
entry.content:
Thanks Holger!

We were able to successfully implement this. Just want to add some notes in case it helps others:

  • The tech user created in this case techUser@example.com has to be added as a "Space Developer" with the email and using the proper origin, i.e <ias tenant>-platform or the name you've chosen depending on the trust configuration.
  • The user techUser@example.com has to be created in IAS and in our case had a fictitious email, so we had to manually set "Verify Email" in the corresponding checkbox (details tab of the user in IAS) otherwise we got an error "User Authentication failed: MAIL_NOT_VERIFIED")
  • In our case we use IAS as a proxy to our corporate IDP. In the configuration of the BTP Cockpit app in IAS we're passing an IDP attribute as "Subject Name Identifier" and we were not using a fallback attribute because it was currently not needed for the current scenario. In this authentication scenario for CICD, the user created only exist locally in IAS and not in our IDP so we had to add the Email from IAS as fallback similar as mentioned in 3568567 otherwise we got error "{"error":"validation_failed","error_description":"Unexpected configuration error."}" 

Hope it helps

Diego

entry.title: Re: Lead status auto update based on Qualification level
entry.content:
Hello Dhaval,

This should be a simple workflow setup on every save of lead with your required conditions and action would be field update. Are you having any issues with that or do you have any specific questions pertaining to the workflow?

Julius

entry.title: Re: Can't use created Integration Suite capabilities
entry.content:
go to "manage capabilities"  and make sure your activate them... got to your related btp subaccount and make sure your assigned the right roles for that for example.  1.https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/activating-and-managing-capabilities 
2.https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/configuring-user-access

entry.title: Re: How to maintain the SOBES field for specific combinat...
entry.content: I already know the transaction and it works I opened the OMD9. Then I opened the correct factory SOBSL combination.After that I can not maintain the field anywhere I have to enter a 5 but when I open the F4 selection I don't see a 5.

entry.title: Re: Preventing closing the Job Requisition until all cand...
entry.content:
Hi @RadoslawVolvo ,

This is a common concern in SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting Management when ensuring data integrity and consistent candidate communication before closing job requisitions.

Out of the box, SuccessFactors does not strictly prevent requisition closure if candidates have not been moved to a final status — including rejected ones — unless specific business rules or validations are implemented.

Available Options in Your Case:
1. Business Rule (on Requisition Closure Action)
You can create a Business Rule that prevents the job requisition from being closed if any candidates are still in non-final statuses.

Trigger Point: Rule on JobRequisition object, with condition on status change (when it's being closed).

Logic: Check if any associated candidate applications are still in non-final statuses (i.e., not moved to either of your two "Rejected" statuses or "Hired").

Action: Show a validation error like: “You cannot close this requisition as some candidates are not yet in a final status.”

2. Permission-Based Control (Not Ideal)
Restrict closure rights only to an admin or HR Ops who can review and manually ensure all candidates are moved before closing — more of a governance workaround than a technical solution.

3. Scheduled Audit Report
If you can’t block closure, another option is to run a report on job requisitions closed while having candidates in non-final statuses:

Helps monitor and address gaps in retrospect.

Could also be tied with Alert Notifications or dashboard KPIs for process improvement.

4. Consider Automation via Integration Center or Intelligent Services
You can explore automatic status updates via Integration Center to move all remaining candidates to a specific "Rejected – No Email" status when closure is triggered. This wouldn’t prevent closure, but ensures proper clean-up.

Recommendation for Your Setup:
Given that:

You have two "Rejected" statuses (one sends emails, one does not).

You do not use the system default "Requisition Closed" status for all unprocessed candidates.

Best option is the custom Business Rule approach, which ensures recruiters cannot close a job unless all candidates are in a final status (Hired or either of the two "Rejected" statuses).

Hope this helps!!

Thanks,

kr,

karthick.

entry.title: What tips would you give an organisation planning to adopt Fiori during their S/4HANA transformation
entry.content: I have worked with different Fiori adoption projects during the S/4HANA transformation. I have a checklist of good adoption plans and bad adoption plans. But I would like to know what your takeaways are from your past projects?

Coal Consumption Monthly
Diesel Consumption Monthly (Stat)
Diesel Consumption Monthly (Mob)
Petrol Consumption Monthly

Thanks in Advance

Tams
